author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. viii. 23, ‘And when he was entered into a boat,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. viii. 23, ‘And when he was entered into a boat,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the Lord’s blessing, I will address you upon the lesson of the Holy Gospel which has just been read, and take occasion thereby to exhort you, that against the tempest and waves of this world, faith sleep not in your hearts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For the Lord Christ had not indeed death nor sleep in His power, and peradventure sleep overcame the Almighty One as He was sailing against His will?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye believe this, He is asleep in you; but if Christ be awake in you, your faith is awake.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle saith, “that Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This sleep then of Christ is a sign of a high mystery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The sailors are the souls passing over the world in wood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That ship also was a figure of the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And all, individually indeed are temples of God, and his own heart is the vessel in which each sails; nor can he suffer shipwreck, if his thoughts are only good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast heard an insult, it is the wind; thou art angry, it is a wave.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When therefore the wind blows, and the wave swells, the ship is endangered, the heart is in jeopardy, the heart is tossed to and fro.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou hast heard an insult, thou longest to be avenged; and, lo, avenged thou hast been, and so rejoicing in another’s harm thou hast suffered shipwreck.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why is this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because Christ is asleep in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does this mean, Christ is asleep in thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast forgotten Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Rouse Him up then, call Christ to mind, let Christ awake in thee, give heed to Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What didst thou wish?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To be avenged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou forgotten, that when He was being crucified, He said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who was asleep in thy heart did not wish to be avenged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Awake Him up then, call Him to remembrance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The remembrance of Him is His word; the remembrance of Him is His command.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then wilt thou say if Christ, awake in thee, What manner of man am I, who wish to be avenged!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who am I, who deal out threatenings against another man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I may die perhaps before I am avenged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when at my last breath, inflamed with rage, and thirsting for vengeance, I shall depart out of this body, He will not receive me, who did not wish to be avenged; He will not receive me, who said, “Give, and it shall be given unto you; forgive, and it shall be forgiven you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore will I refrain myself from my wrath, and return to the repose of my heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ hath commanded the sea, tranquillity is restored.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what I have said as to anger, hold fast as a rule in all your temptations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A temptation has sprung up; it is the wind; thou art disturbed; it is a wave.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Awake up Christ then, let Him speak with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who is this, since the winds and the sea obey Him?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is this, whom the sea obeyeth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The sea is His, and He made it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Imitate the winds then, and the sea rather; obey the Creator.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At Christ’s command the sea giveth ear; and art thou deaf?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The sea heareth, and the wind ceaseth: and dost thou still blow on?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I say, I do, I devise; what is all this, but to be blowing on, and to be unwilling to stop in obedience to the word of Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not the wave master you in this troubled state of your heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet since we are but men, if the wind should drive us on, and stir up the affections of our souls, let us not despair; let us awake Christ, that we may sail on a tranquil sea, and so come to our country.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let us turn to the Lord,” etc.
